mission.title: pushgateway: Don't accept timestamps from pushes
mission.description: Prometheus Pushgateway rejects timestamps in pushed metrics to prevent misuse. Users pushing timestamps were typically trying to use Prometheus as a push-based system, which is incorrect. Since Pushgateway v0.5.0, pushed metrics with timestamps are rejected by default.

steps.title: Check your Pushgateway version
steps.description:
Verify you are running Pushgateway v0.5.0 or later, which includes timestamp rejection:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n monitoring -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus-pushgateway
kubectl exec -n monitoring deploy/prometheus-pushgateway -- /bin/pushgateway --version 2>/dev/null
```
If running an older version, upgrade to v0.5.0+ to get this fix.

steps.title: Understand the behavior change
steps.description:
Starting with v0.5.0, the Pushgateway returns an HTTP 400 error if pushed metrics contain timestamps. This is by design — the Pushgateway manages its own timestamps based on when metrics are pushed.

If your client is sending timestamps, you need to update it to omit timestamps from the Prometheus text exposition format. For example:
```
# WRONG: includes timestamp
some_metric 42 1541051096000

# CORRECT: no timestamp
some_metric 42
```

steps.title: Verify timestamp rejection works
steps.description:
Test that timestamps are correctly rejected:
```bash
# This should return HTTP 400
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --data-binary @- http://localhost:9091/metrics/job/test <<'EOF'
some_metric 42 1541051096000
EOF

# This should return HTTP 200
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --data-binary @- http://localhost:9091/metrics/job/test <<'EOF'
some_metric 42
EOF
```

resolution.summary: Pushgateway v0.5.0+ rejects pushed metrics containing timestamps with an HTTP 400 error. This prevents misuse where users incorrectly tried to push timestamped metrics (effectively trying to make Prometheus a push-based system). The fix was implemented in PR #119.

prerequisites.description: A running Prometheus Pushgateway deployment (typically in a monitoring namespace).
